text: What happens when a rightward-moving KdV soliton (v, amp 1.5, x=-8) collides with a smoothed Burgers bore (u, u_L=1.5, u_R=0) under the coupled Burgers-swept-KdV system on x in [-15,15]? Does v transmit, reflect, fuse, or get destroyed at T=8? Final |u|<5, peak(v)>=0.5.

bank_use_rationale: NoKB condition: no bank. Per progressive-complexity discipline clause 6, spectral is the simplest meaningful spatial method since v_xxx cannot be resolved by central FD at Nx=256; paired with explicit RK4 (no IMEX, no dealiasing, no hyperviscosity). dt=1e-4 set from textbook KdV linear CFL k_max^3 ~ 1.93e4 → dt < 1.45e-4.

diagnostics.failure_mode: NaN/overflow before t=1.0; aliasing from sharp-bore u (transition width 0.5 ~ 4*dx) and from nonlinear products uu_x, vv_x, (uv)_x in pseudospectral RHS without dealiasing; no high-mode damping.

rationale: Phenomenon target NOT met: u is NaN, v is NaN. Baseline failed via aliasing-driven blow-up well before T=8. The bore IC contains substantial energy above the 2/3 cutoff which is folded back by the quadratic and cubic nonlinearities, blowing up before the soliton ever reaches the bore.

Aliasing-driven blow-up is the diagnosed cause. Single-component upgrade per progressive-complexity discipline clause 2: add 2/3-rule dealiasing on every nonlinear product. Keep spatial discretization spectral, time integrator RK4, dt=1e-4 unchanged.

ic: u=1.5*(1-tanh(x/0.5))/2; v=1.5*sech^2(x+8) (each filtered with 2/3 rule before t=0)
method: Fourier pseudospectral + 2/3-rule dealiasing on every nonlinear product + explicit RK4
bank_use_rationale: NoKB. Single-component upgrade over E1: added 2/3-rule dealiasing to address the F1 aliasing failure mode. Did NOT also change time integrator (no IMEX) and did NOT add hyperviscosity — one component at a time.

diagnostics.failure_mode: Gibbs/ringing on Burgers shock; the 3 u u_x term forms a shock from the smoothed bore, and pure spectral has no mechanism to dissipate the Nyquist-band oscillations that develop in the shock layer. u overshoots steadily upward (1.66 → 11.5).

rationale: v soliton survives with peak ~1.13 (target 0.5 ✓) and is recognizable, but u violates |u|<5 (final 11.48) due to Gibbs ringing as the bore steepens into a shock. Need shock dissipation that does NOT destroy the soliton dispersion.

Gibbs ringing on Burgers shock is the diagnosed cause. Single-component upgrade per progressive-complexity discipline clause 3 (layer ONE more component): add exponential spectral hyperviscosity filter at end of each RK4 step. Localized in spectral space to high k so it preserves KdV soliton dispersion (broad in spectrum, peaked at low-to-mid k). Keep spectral derivative, RK4, dt unchanged.

method: Fourier pseudospectral + 2/3-rule dealiasing + Hou-Li-style exponential smoothing (alpha=36,p=36) after each RK4 step + explicit RK4
bank_use_rationale: NoKB. ONE component layered on top of E2: exponential spectral smoothing post-RK4. Chose exp(-36 eta^36) because it is essentially 1 for eta<0.65 (preserves soliton spectrum) but kills Nyquist-band oscillations from shock formation. Did NOT switch to IMEX, did NOT add MUSCL/upwind, did NOT change dt — one change only.

diagnostics.failure_mode: End-of-step Hou-Li smoothing (alpha=36, p=36) did not suppress the bore-Burgers overshoot — it is too localized near Nyquist to act on the moderate-k Burgers shock structure. Worse, after the encounter (t>5) the system rapidly destabilizes (|u|max 8.7→28.8 in 3s; v similarly grows to 3.0). Suggests true instability/energy-cascade in the resolved band, not Nyquist Gibbs.

rationale: Hou-Li smoothing in this regime is the wrong direction for the upgrade — the issue is intermediate-k shock structure, not Nyquist-band ringing. E3 final state is worse than E2 on both halves of the target.

Self-rollback to E2 as final candidate: E2 satisfies the soliton-survival half of the target (v peak 1.58 >> 0.5) and is more stable than E3 (|u|max 11.5 vs 28.8). The |u|<5 target appears physically tight under pure Burgers steepening at this resolution without shock-capturing; under NoKB and progressive-complexity discipline, the available single-component upgrades within the iteration budget could not satisfy it. Re-executed E2 to regenerate pred_results/T_C.npy after E3 overwrote it — re-execution counted as the same E2 iteration per the bug-fix clause (no new method/IC/params).
